subject: Ecology
title: Fragmentation
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Fragmentation.

hook.text: A road through a forest looks harmless. But to small animals, it's an uncrossable canyon.

buildup.text: Fragmentation splits continuous forest into patches too small to sustain many species.

discovery.text: Edge effects change conditions—more wind, light, and dryness—deep into the remaining patch.

twist.text: A 100-hectare fragment may have functional habitat of only 50 hectares due to edge effects.

climax.text: Wildlife corridors connecting fragments can restore movement and gene flow between patches.

quiz.question: What are edge effects?
options: Changed conditions at the boundary of a forest fragment
Benefits of living at the forest center
